steps.title: Block the oval (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a tilted oval — wider than tall, leaning a touch to one side. Don't aim for perfect; lemons are lumpy little ovals, not eggs.

steps.title: Mark the two nubs (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, add a small pointed bump at each end of the oval — the stem nub and the blossom nub. Keep them gentle, more nudge than spike.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the full outline in one confident pass, including both little nubs. Let the line wobble slightly — citrus skin is bumpy, and a too-clean line reads as plastic.

steps.title: Suggest the texture (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add three or four short dot or tick marks scattered loosely across the surface. Don't cover the whole lemon — a handful of marks is enough to suggest the dimpled rind.

steps.title: Anchor with a shadow line (ink)
steps.description: Add a single short horizontal mark just under the lemon where it meets the counter. One small stroke is all it takes to settle it on the surface.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove the pencil guides where they distract. The lemon is small — keep the ink reading clearly.
